behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (HTTP raw-body extraction -> signature verifier -> enqueue path). A Unit test of verifier logic can pass while handler wires parsed body instead of raw bytes. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process app wiring is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss a bug where the handler verifies one payload representation but forwards another. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real network conditions or OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler in-process with real middleware/body parsing and a spy/fake queue module. Send a request with a deliberately invalid X-Signature-256 over a known raw body; assert HTTP 401 and assert enqueue was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is ordering across modules (handler response timing vs queue persistence completion). Unit-level branch tests can miss missing await/transaction commit ordering. REAL-INFRA: needs real queue persistence module (e.g., local DB/filesystem) but not real external network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss bug where enqueue Promise is fired and response returns before durable write finishes. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior does not require real internet HTTP or OS-specific primitives beyond local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP test against the real queue persistence adapter (local test DB/filesystem). Make enqueue artificially slow at commit point; assert response is not sent until durable write completes, then 200 is returned.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in orchestration between enqueue/dispatch and response path. Unit testing only processing function misses handler waiting on downstream completion. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; controlled in-process slow worker is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss accidental await on processing completion in handler path. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because real network stack is not required to detect this wait-vs-no-wait bug.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with a downstream processor that blocks on a Promise. Send request in-process; assert HTTP response returns quickly while processor Promise is still unresolved, then resolve and verify processing finishes later.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is wall-clock SLA under concurrency and HTTP serving behavior; real event loop scheduling, socket handling, and timer behavior matter. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes/fake timers can hide queue contention/event-loop starvation seen only under real server/runtime conditions. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration with mocked time can miss real latency spikes from concurrent requests and Node runtime scheduling. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly requires wall-clock latency under concurrent load.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real Node HTTP server on a test port. Preload slow concurrent downstream work, then send webhook requests concurrently over real HTTP and measure end-to-end latency; assert each acknowledgment is <=3s while background processing remains slow.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedup correctness is at boundary between handler idempotency logic and persistent queue/storage uniqueness. Unit logic tests can miss race/constraint integration bugs. REAL-INFRA: requires real local persistence behavior but not external OS/network beyond local DB/filesystem. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss bug where duplicate check is in memory only and second process/request still inserts duplicate. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because real external network/subprocess is not required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process handler with real persistence-backed queue. Submit same event ID twice (including close timing). Assert first is stored and second is rejected/ignored, and only one queue record exists.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior depends on contract between queue module's 'full' signal and HTTP translation (status + header). A Unit mock can hide mismatch in real error/return shape. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; module wiring suffices. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss handler failing to recognize actual queue-full error emitted by queue module, returning wrong status/header. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about queue signal mapping, not real network/OS failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to real queue interface implementation configured at capacity (or returning its real full error object). Send webhook in-process; assert HTTP 503 and `Retry-After` header is present with expected value.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this verifies real persistence/capacity enforcement across queue logic + storage, not just algorithmic counting. Unit fakes can pass while real adapter ignores limits. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is enough; no real external network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss bug where capacity config is not applied in storage layer and inserts continue past limit. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require external OS/network/subprocess interactions beyond local persistence integration.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a small configured capacity in test storage. Enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more; assert overflow is rejected with the queue's full error and persisted count never exceeds configured capacity.
